data_contracts_notes: No data structures, API contracts, or schema changes described. Not applicable to this rule-porting task.

CONTAINED: Source: Ported from Next.js ESLint plugin
Rule scope: next/script components with inline content or dangerouslySetInnerHTML props
Rule applicability: both app dir and pages dir
MISSING_or_PARTIAL: No specific test cases or examples (Given/When/Then)
No explicit error handling requirements described
No edge cases mentioned (e.g., what counts as 'inline content', nested scripts, conditional rendering)
No acceptance criteria beyond 'port the rule'
No data contract details (what does the rule report? what metadata?)
Behavioral specificity low: vague on what 'enforces id attribute' means (error? warning? auto-fix?)
No scope boundaries clarifying what code paths/scenarios the rule does/doesn't cover
No definition of when rule should/shouldn't trigger
No mention of configuration options (if any)

observations: This is a porting task with external references (Next.js docs, ESLint source). The spec relies heavily on readers following links to understand requirements. Outcome is directionally clear ('port this rule') but behavioral details are implicit in the linked Next.js rule, not explicitly stated here. A developer would need to read the ESLint source to understand triggering conditions, error messages, and auto-fix behavior. No acceptance criteria are testable from this spec alone.

scope_boundaries_notes: Clearly a bugfix to a specific linter rule. In-scope: fixing the newline insertion bug. Out-of-scope: other linter rules or refactoring.
acceptance_criteria_notes: Has a concrete before/after example showing the bug. Expected result is stated. Missing: explicit test cases or validation steps.
data_contracts_notes: Not applicable; this is a formatting/code generation fix, not a data shape change.
dependency_context_notes: References the specific rule (`useReadonlyClassProperties`), the issue number (#7948), and the problematic enum variant (`AnyJsClassMemberName::JsLiteralMemberName`). Missing: file paths or module structure where the fix was applied.
behavioral_specificity_notes: Concrete example showing exact buggy output (newline placement). Root cause identified (trivia retention). Missing: boundary conditions—does this fix work for all member types, or only `JsLiteralMemberName`?
